detailLines: Show detailed help for a front-end-local command.
Examples: /help /time, /help /world style, /help tp
Tip: Use the Help button or /world help to see the command list.
Read or set the local world time without sending a Codex request.
Accepted inputs: normalized cycle (0..1), hour (0..24), clock time (for example 6:30am), percent (for example 75%), or a preset.
Presets: dawn, sunrise, morning, noon, afternoon, sunset, dusk, evening, night, midnight
Examples: /time noon, /time 18.5, /time 6:30am, /time 75%
Sends a local repo commit request to the Codex backend route.
Use /commit <message hint> to suggest commit wording.
If no backend is connected, the command will not create a commit locally by itself.
Teleport the player to a matching biome in the current world.
Specify coordinates instead of a biome to teleport directly.
The slash form and bare alias both work.
Example: /tp forest or /tp 1234 -567
Show front-end-local world command help and available biome names.
Aliases: /world commands, /world ?
Use /help /world style for detailed style command help.
Teleport to a biome by name using the /world command namespace.
Example: /world biome forest
Explicit teleport form for biome travel.
Equivalent intent to /world biome <biome-name>.
Show or set the terrain detail overlay render distance (in meters/world units).
Biome-specific close-range detail is faded out beyond this distance.
Adjust the intensity to amplify or soften the biome-specific detail textures.
Examples: /world detail 50, /world detail off, /world detail distance 80, /world detail intensity 1.5
Set a biome style override color for terrain, water, fog, tree trunk, or tree canopy.
Color must be in #rrggbb format.
Example: /world style forest terrain #335f2d
Alternate /world style form that explicitly targets tree trunk or canopy colors.
Example: /world style forest tree canopy #3f8144
Clear biome style overrides for one target or all targets.
The code also accepts 'reset' as an alias for 'clear'.
Examples: /world style clear forest, /world style clear forest fog
Confirms a pending front-end world command clarification prompt.
Declines a pending front-end world command clarification prompt.
